title: Callistemon montis-zamiae
taxonomy: Callistemon montis-zamiae is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to New South Wales in Australia, and is a member of the Callistemon genus. The genus Callistemon is commonly known as the bottlebrush due to the shape of its flowers. Callistemon montis-zamiae is a unique species within this genus, and is often found in dry forests and woodlands. The exact classification of Callistemon montis-zamiae is as follows: Callistemon montis-zamiae Rye, 1995, a species within the family Myrtaceae.

intro: Callistemon montis-zamiae, also known as the Zamia Bottlebrush, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to Western Australia. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in Aboriginal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Bioactive constituents of Callistemon montis-zamiae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
